rubric_items.reasoning: The agent didn't just trust the (accurate) premise that the environment was already fixed — it re-queried live cluster state for all three success criteria (job completion status, PVC claimName, log directory env var, RoleBinding namespace) before concluding. Oracle confirms pass, and the checks performed are the same ones a fresh state-check would need, so the verification is genuine and would hold up on a re-check, not a lucky assumption.

Single, direct diagnostic pass: one bash invocation covering every relevant resource (jobs, history-server pod, PVC reference, log dir, rolebinding subject namespace), followed immediately by a correct conclusion. No backtracking, no redundant exploration, no unnecessary stages.

Used only scoped, read-only kubectl get/jsonpath queries targeted at the exact namespaces and resources named in the task (spark-team-a, spark-team-b, spark-history). No mutating commands were issued since none were needed, matching the K8s activity log of 8 reads / 0 mutations.
Zero mutations, one batched read-only command, no collateral changes. This is the minimal possible footprint for confirming an already-correct state — no over-verification, no unrelated touches.
